Defendants' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID P. NEMECEK, JR., ESQ. of Beugelmans, LLP, Tel No. 415-856-9174 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JENNIFER RUSNAK, ESQ. of G&P\Schick, Tel No. 408-995-5050 appearing for Defendants Elajou Group, LLC, Juma S. Elajou, and Broadway87CDEV, LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Plaintiff's plausible interpretation of the Financing Agreement, as alleged in the first amended complaint, is accepted as correct on a pleadings motion. (Weil and Brown, Civil Procedure Before Trial Guide at 7:48:25). Although paragraph 8 states that the agreement will remain effective for one year, paragraph 1 states that plaintiff is engaged "on an exclusive basis . . . commencing on January 15th, 2012, and expiring at midnight on January 15 2014. Nor is there is anything in paragraph 8 that refers to or limits plaintiff's entitlement to "Payment Due upon Flip." As to defendants Mr. Elajou and Broadway87CDEV, the motion fails because plaintiff alleges that they are alter egos of defendant Elajou Group. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID P. NEMECEK, JR., ESQ. of Beugelmans, LLP, Tel No. 415-856-9174 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JENNIFER RUSNAK, ESQ. of G&P/Schnick, Tel No. 408-995-5050 appearing for Defendants.

Both sides stipulate to matter being heard by pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF REGENCY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC'S Motion to Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And First Set Of Form Interrogatories By Defendants Elajou Group, Llc, Juma Elajou And Broadway 87CDEV, LLC And For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $4,800, the Motion is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. First, as a preliminary issue, the Court notes that RCP filed and served its separate statements and declarations on December 23, 2015 in violation of the ex parte order shortening time on this Motion. However, the Elajou defendants have not presented any evidence that they were prejudiced by this one-day delay. The Elajou defendants argue that RCP failed to properly meet and confer before filing this Motion. However, no verifications were provided to any of the responses, raising the issue of whether a meet and confer effort was required at least as to the responses to interrogatories. Form Interrogatories: 2.1 (entity defendants): denied. 2.3-7 (Juma S. Elajou): granted. 12.2 (all defendants): granted, responding party shall answer all subparts. 15.1: denied, defendants answered the interrogatory. RFP: all attorney-client privilege objections are granted, the Elajou defendants shall provide a privilege log within two weeks of this order. As to the specific requests: 1: denied as overbroad. 2: granted. 3: granted, limited to operating agreements and documents filed with the CA Secretary of State. 4: granted, limited to meetings of minutes, resolutions, organizational charts, capitalization tables, lists of members and managing members. 5: granted. 6: granted, limited to transfer of assets by responding party to Elajou Grou and/or Broadway87C Dev. 7: denied as overbroad. 8: granted. 9-10: denied as overbroad. 11-12: granted. 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19, 20, 22, 32, 33, 41: granted, limited to communications regarding the sale of the Subject Property. 17, 23: granted. 21: denied as overbroad. 24: denied. 25-26: denied. 27: granted, subject to any protective order in the Christine Hughes Litigation. 28: granted, subject to any protective order in the Marcus & Millichap Litigation. 29: denied, over broad. 30: denied, overbroad. 30: granted. 34: granted as to the purchase agreement and amendments only. 35-36: denied. 37-38: granted. 39, 40: denied. 42: granted. 43: granted, limited to meetings of minutes, resolutions, organizational charts, capitalization tables, lists of members and managing members. Documents containing private, personal information (i.e. SSN, the income of individuals, tax information shall be redacted). Amended responses and responsive documents shall be served within two weeks of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. Moving party shall prepare and submit proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: Sean Kane, Reporter: Wendy C. RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS COMPLAINANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC and JUMA ELAJOU's Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Post An Undertaking is denied. MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC & JUMA ELAJOU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. As to the first, fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action, the demurrer is overruled because it is not apparent from the face of the complaint that Plaintiff is required to be licensed as a real estate broker.
